sections.paragraph:
νιπτήρ, ῆρος, ὁ (νίπτω; cp. Hdt. 2, 172 ποδανιπτήρ; a Cyprian ins fr. Roman times has the acc. νιπτῆρα: ASakellarios, Τα Κυπριακα I 1890, p. 191 no. 2=GDI 123, 8; Lex. Vindob. p. 128, 16; TestAbr A 3 p. 80, 11 [Stone p. 8; s. NTS 1, ’54, 220]; TestJob 25:6) (wash) basin J 13:5 (ποδονιπτήρ P66).—DELG s.v. νίζω.

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

Cyprian Cyprian , III a.d.—List 5

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

  1. fr. = from

acc. acc. = accusative

GDI GDI = Sammlung der griechischen Dialekt-Inschriften—List 3

TestAbr TestAbr = Testament of Abraham, with some interpolations; I b.c./I a.d.—Lists 2, 5

NTS NTS = New Testament Studies—List 6

TestJob TestJob = Testament of Job—List 2

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 674
